chunks.title: Yahweh's Charge to Joshua
chunks.reason: Divine commission and encouragement for leadership.

chunks.title: Joshua's Orders to the Officers and Tribes
chunks.reason: Narrative of Joshua instructing officers and addressing the Reubenites, Gadites, and half-tribe of Manasseh.

chunks.title: The Tribes' Response to Joshua
chunks.reason: Verbal agreement and oath of loyalty from the tribes.

chunks.title: Spies Enter Jericho and Hide
chunks.reason: Describes the arrival of the spies, their lodging with Rahab, and her initial deception of the king's men.

chunks.title: Rahab's Confession and Request
chunks.reason: Contains Rahab's speech declaring faith in Yahweh and her plea for the safety of her family.

chunks.title: The Agreement and Escape Plan
chunks.reason: Details the physical escape via the window, the terms of the scarlet cord, and the spies' departure.

chunks.title: Return to Joshua
chunks.reason: Concludes the narrative with the spies hiding for three days and reporting back to Joshua.

chunks.title: Crossing the Jordan Begins
chunks.reason: Describes the movement of Israel to the Jordan and Joshua's initial commands to the people and priests.

chunks.title: God's Promise and Instructions
chunks.reason: Combines Yahweh's oracle to Joshua with Joshua's exhortation and specific instructions regarding the miracle.

chunks.title: The Waters Divide
chunks.reason: Narrates the fulfillment of the promise as the priests enter the water and the people cross on dry ground.

chunks.title: Gathering Stones and Crossing Completion
chunks.reason: Narrative of Joshua's command to gather stones, the completion of the crossing, and the arrival at Gilgal.

chunks.title: Priests Exit the Jordan
chunks.reason: Direct exchange between Yahweh and Joshua regarding the priests coming up from the river.

chunks.title: Setting Up Stones at Gilgal
chunks.reason: Account of setting up stones and Joshua's speech explaining the miracle to future generations.

chunks.title: Fear of the Kings and Circumcision Command
chunks.reason: Narrative introduction of enemy reaction followed by divine instruction to Joshua.

chunks.title: Circumcision at Gibeath-haaraloth and Gilgal
chunks.reason: Execution of the command with historical explanation and renaming of the place.

chunks.title: Passover and Cessation of Manna
chunks.reason: Chronological account of the first Passover in Canaan and the end of manna.

chunks.title: Appearance of the Commander of Yahweh
chunks.reason: Joshua encounters a divine figure who commands him to remove his sandals.

chunks.title: God's Instructions for Jericho
chunks.reason: Describes the initial divine command and specific tactical instructions given to Joshua.

chunks.title: The Six Days of Marching
chunks.reason: Narrates the execution of the first six days where the army marched around the city once daily.

chunks.title: The Seventh Day and Conquest
chunks.reason: Covers the climax of the event with seven marches, the shout, the wall falling, and the city's destruction.

chunks.title: Rahab and Her Family Saved
chunks.reason: Details the specific rescue of Rahab's household and the burning of the city.

chunks.title: The Curse on Jericho
chunks.reason: Records Joshua's oath regarding the future rebuilding of the city and concludes with Yahweh's presence.

chunks.title: Achan's Sin and the Defeat at Ai
chunks.reason: Narrative unit describing the cause of defeat and the immediate military loss.

chunks.title: Joshua's Lament
chunks.reason: Joshua's emotional response and prayer to God regarding the defeat.

chunks.title: God's Response and Instructions
chunks.reason: Divine oracle explaining the cause of failure and prescribing the method of judgment.

chunks.title: Identification of Achan
chunks.reason: Narrative sequence of the tribal selection process and discovery of the culprit.

chunks.title: Judgment and Resolution
chunks.reason: Conclusion of the narrative with the execution of Achan and restoration of God's favor.

chunks.title: Yahweh's Command and Joshua's Preparation
chunks.reason: Divine instruction followed by the strategic deployment of troops.

chunks.title: The Ambush Strategy Unfolds
chunks.reason: Narrative description of the army positioning and the enemy's pursuit.

chunks.title: The Capture and Destruction of Ai
chunks.reason: Climactic battle action, the city's burning, and the execution of the king.

chunks.title: The Covenant Ceremony at Mount Ebal
chunks.reason: Ritual observance involving altar construction, sacrifice, and the reading of the Law.

chunks.title: Kings Gather to Fight Israel
chunks.reason: Describes the political reaction of surrounding kings to Joshua's victories.

chunks.title: Gibeonites Deceive Israel
chunks.reason: Narrates the Gibeonites' strategy to appear as distant travelers and secure a covenant.

chunks.title: Discovery of Deception and Resolution
chunks.reason: Details the discovery of the Gibeonites' proximity and the leaders' decision to spare them.

chunks.title: Joshua Confronts the Gibeonites
chunks.reason: Contains Joshua's questioning and the Gibeonites' explanation of their motive.

chunks.title: The Five Kings Alliance
chunks.reason: Describes the gathering of five Amorite kings to attack Gibeon.

chunks.title: Joshua's Victory and the Sun Stands Still
chunks.reason: Covers Gibeon's plea, Joshua's march, divine intervention, and the miraculous day.

chunks.title: The Five Kings Captured
chunks.reason: Details the hiding of kings in a cave and their subsequent execution.

chunks.title: Conquest of Southern Cities
chunks.reason: Lists the sequential capture and destruction of Makkedah, Libnah, Lachish, Eglon, Hebron, and Debir.

chunks.title: Summary of the Campaign
chunks.reason: Summarizes the total conquest of the land and return to Gilgal.

chunks.title: The Northern Alliance Assembles
chunks.reason: Describes the gathering of northern kings and their armies at Merom.

chunks.title: Divine Command and Battle Victory
chunks.reason: Contains Yahweh's oracle to Joshua followed by the execution of the battle.

chunks.title: Destruction of Hazor and Cities
chunks.reason: Details the capture of Hazor, the destruction of its inhabitants, and the taking of plunder.

chunks.title: Summary of Conquest and Divine Hardening
chunks.reason: Summarizes the extent of the land taken and explains the theological reason for the total destruction.

chunks.title: Elimination of Anakim and Final Rest
chunks.reason: Describes the removal of the Anakim and concludes with the land being given to Israel.

chunks.title: Kings East of Jordan
chunks.reason: Catalog of kings defeated by Moses east of the Jordan River.

chunks.title: Kings West of Jordan
chunks.reason: Catalog of kings defeated by Joshua west of the Jordan River.

chunks.title: Yahweh's Charge to Joshua
chunks.reason: Divine instruction regarding remaining land and the command to apportion it.

chunks.title: Inheritance of Reuben, Gad, and Levi
chunks.reason: Summary of the eastern territories given by Moses and the specific case of Levi.

chunks.title: Territory of Reuben
chunks.reason: Detailed boundaries and cities for the tribe of Reuben.

chunks.title: Territory of Gad
chunks.reason: Detailed boundaries and cities for the tribe of Gad.

chunks.title: Territory of Manasseh
chunks.reason: Detailed boundaries and cities for the half-tribe of Manasseh.

chunks.title: Conclusion on Levi
chunks.reason: Final statement confirming the Levitical inheritance as Yahweh Himself.

chunks.title: Overview of Land Division
chunks.reason: Introductory summary describing the apportionment process and tribal allocations.

chunks.title: Caleb's Request for Hebron
chunks.reason: A continuous speech by Caleb recounting his history and requesting the hill country.

chunks.title: Grant of Hebron to Caleb
chunks.reason: Narrative conclusion detailing Joshua's blessing and the historical context of Hebron.

chunks.title: Border of Judah
chunks.reason: Describes the geographical boundaries of the tribe of Judah.

chunks.title: Caleb's Inheritance and Othniel
chunks.reason: Narrative of Caleb receiving Hebron and Othniel capturing Debir.

chunks.title: Southern Cities
chunks.reason: Catalog of cities in the southern region of Judah.

chunks.title: Shephelah Cities
chunks.reason: Catalog of cities in the lowland region of Judah.

chunks.title: Hill Country Cities
chunks.reason: Catalog of cities in the mountainous region of Judah.

chunks.title: Wilderness and Jerusalem Note
chunks.reason: List of wilderness cities followed by the note on the Jebusites.

chunks.title: Lot for Joseph
chunks.reason: Describes the geographical boundaries of the inheritance for the sons of Joseph.

chunks.title: Territory of Ephraim
chunks.reason: Details the specific borders of Ephraim's inheritance and notes the remaining Canaanite population.

chunks.title: Inheritance of Manasseh and Zelophehad's Daughters
chunks.reason: Describes the allotment of land to Manasseh and the specific case of daughters receiving inheritance.

chunks.title: Boundaries and Cities of Manasseh
chunks.reason: Details the geographical borders and lists specific cities held by the tribe.

chunks.title: Joshua's Instruction to Joseph
chunks.reason: Records a conversation between the sons of Joseph and Joshua regarding their inheritance.

chunks.title: Assembly at Shiloh and Land Survey
chunks.reason: Describes the gathering of Israel, Joshua's command to survey the land, and the casting of lots.

chunks.title: Territory of Benjamin
chunks.reason: Details the geographical borders of the tribe of Benjamin as assigned by lot.

chunks.title: Cities of Benjamin
chunks.reason: Lists the specific cities and villages belonging to the tribe of Benjamin.

chunks.title: Inheritance of Simeon
chunks.reason: Describes the allocation of cities and villages to the tribe of Simeon within Judah's territory.

chunks.title: Inheritance of Zebulun
chunks.reason: Details the borders and specific cities assigned to the tribe of Zebulun.

chunks.title: Inheritance of Issachar
chunks.reason: Lists the territory and cities given to the tribe of Issachar.

chunks.title: Inheritance of Asher
chunks.reason: Describes the coastal borders and cities assigned to the tribe of Asher.

chunks.title: Inheritance of Naphtali
chunks.reason: Outlines the northern borders and fortified cities for the tribe of Naphtali.

chunks.title: Inheritance of Dan
chunks.reason: Lists initial cities and includes a narrative about the tribe's expansion to Leshem.

chunks.title: Completion of Land Division
chunks.reason: Concludes the distribution process and notes Joshua's specific inheritance.

chunks.title: Command to Designate Cities of Refuge
chunks.reason: Yahweh's direct instruction to Joshua regarding the establishment and function of cities of refuge.

chunks.title: Designation of Specific Cities
chunks.reason: A catalog listing the six specific cities assigned to their respective tribes.

chunks.title: Summary of Purpose
chunks.reason: Concluding statement defining the scope and purpose of the appointed cities for all Israelites.

chunks.title: Levites Request Cities
chunks.reason: Narrative introduction of the Levites requesting cities and the initial allocation by lot.

chunks.title: Cities for Aaron's Sons
chunks.reason: Detailed list of thirteen cities given to the priestly families from Judah, Simeon, and Benjamin.

chunks.title: Cities for Kohathite Families
chunks.reason: List of ten cities allotted to the remaining Kohathite families from Ephraim, Dan, and Manasseh.

chunks.title: Cities for Gershonites
chunks.reason: List of thirteen cities given to the Gershonite families from Manasseh, Issachar, Asher, and Naphtali.

chunks.title: Cities for Merari Families
chunks.reason: List of twelve cities assigned to the Merari families from Zebulun, Reuben, and Gad.

chunks.title: Summary of Completion
chunks.reason: Concluding summary of the total cities given and the fulfillment of God's promises to Israel.

chunks.title: Dismissal of the Eastern Tribes
chunks.reason: Joshua sends the Reubenites, Gadites, and half-tribe of Manasseh back to their land with blessings.

chunks.title: The Altar and the Accusation
chunks.reason: The tribes build an altar causing Israel to assemble for war, followed by Phinehas's delegation delivering an accusation.

chunks.title: The Explanation and Resolution
chunks.reason: The eastern tribes explain the altar's purpose as a witness, leading to Phinehas's acceptance and the end of the conflict.

chunks.title: Joshua's Call and Inheritance
chunks.reason: Narrative introduction of Joshua's age and the distribution of remaining lands.

chunks.title: Call to Faithfulness
chunks.reason: Direct instructions to keep the law and warnings against intermarriage.

chunks.title: Covenant Warning
chunks.reason: Joshua's final testimony regarding God's faithfulness and future judgment.

chunks.title: Joshua's Covenant Speech at Shechem
chunks.reason: A continuous speech by Joshua recounting history and calling for covenant renewal, including the people's response.

chunks.title: Deaths of Joshua, Joseph, and Eleazar
chunks.reason: Narrative account of the deaths and burials of key leaders concluding the chapter.
